I'll search for this paper to verify the exact title and author information.
The paper absolutely exists and is well-documented across multiple authoritative sources. The exact title matches the cited reference, and the search results confirm the author names. The paper was published at NeurIPS (Neural Information Processing Systems) in 2017, which matches the "Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems" venue cited.

The author name discrepancy noted by the automated checkers is a minor formatting/encoding issue: the cited reference shows "Kristof Sch ̈utt" (with a malformed umlaut character), while the actual paper shows "Kristof T. Schütt" (with the proper full middle initial "T." and correct umlaut). This is a character encoding or OCR artifact in the cited metadata, not evidence of hallucination—the cited name is a corrupted version of the actual author name, which is a common metadata extraction error rather than AI fabrication.

EXPLANATION: The paper exists and was published at NeurIPS 2017 with the exact cited title. The author name discrepancy is a minor character encoding error (malformed umlaut in the cited version versus properly formatted "Kristof T. Schütt" in the actual paper), not evidence of hallucination.

LINK: https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.08566

FOUND_TITLE: SchNet: A continuous-filter convolutional neural network for modeling quantum interactions

FOUND_AUTHORS: Kristof T. Schütt, Pieter-Jan Kindermans, Huziel Enoc Sauceda Felix, Stefan Chmiela, Alexandre Tkatchenko, Klaus-Robert Müller

FOUND_YEAR: 2017
